title: Te Pari Acquires NZ Sheep Handler Brand Combi Clamp
description_clean: Oamaru-based livestock handling specialist Te Pari has bought Combi Clamp, the New Zealand manufacturer of Combi Clamp manual sheep handlers. Launched in 2002, Combi Clamp has earned a strong reputation across NZ and Australia for producing reliable manual sheep handling equipment to help farmers work more efficiently, while improving animal welfare. The addition of the brand will expand Te Pari’s sheep handling offering and complement its existing range of Racewell Sheep solutions. The product will be rebranded as the Te Pari Combi Clamp and integrated into an established sales and support ne
summary: Oamaru-based Te Pari has acquired Combi Clamp, the New Zealand manufacturer of manual sheep handlers launched in 2002, and will rebrand the product as the Te Pari Combi Clamp.

title: Sire's grandmother inspires interstate stud raid at 81st Glen Innes Hereford sale

title: Police rescue team phones in local farmer, who saves elderly man and dog
description_clean: Rescuers had grave concerns for an elderly fisherman stranded at night in the cold by a South Island river, until they called in a farmer with local knowledge who was able to locate the man in the dark.
summary: A South Island farmer with local knowledge was called in by a police rescue team and located an elderly stranded fisherman and his dog in the dark by a river.

title: Toyota RAV4 Plug-in Hybrid Arrives in New Zealand
description_clean: While hybrids and PHEVs have not usually been the focus of farmers and rural dwellers, the current Iranian-driven fuel crisis has pushed up fuel costs and created a new interest in the genres. The Toyota RAV4 is already well known and seen on many rural driveways, being New Zealand’s most popular vehicle. Kiwi drivers will now have a practical, no-compromise choice of electrification with the addition of the first-ever Toyota RAV4 Plug-in Hybrid (PHEV). Said to blend electric driving for everyday use with the flexibility and confidence of Toyota’s proven hybrid technology, the company suggests
summary: Toyota has launched its first-ever RAV4 Plug-in Hybrid in New Zealand, offering electrification amid rising fuel costs, with the RAV4 already the country's most popular vehicle.

title: Aussie beef excluded from latest 12.5pc US tariff hike
description_clean: Australian beef has been excluded, while lamb and goatmeat are included in the latest round of punitive tariffs on imported products announced by the US overnight. Effective today, the Trump administration has imposed a 12.5pc tariff on a wide range of Australian goods after accusing the Federal Government of failing to adequately police forced labour in its supply chains
summary: The Trump administration has imposed a 12.5pc tariff on a wide range of Australian goods over forced labour concerns, including lamb and goatmeat, while Australian beef was excluded.

title: Japan Wagyu tour 2027 promises to expand Aussie breeders’ knowledge
description_clean: About 40 Australian beef industry stakeholders with an interest in Wagyu cattle and beef production will depart next August on a 12-day AWA study tour designed to further knowledge and understanding of the Japanese Wagyu supply chain – from genetics to breeding, feeding, processing, and consumer applications in Japan’s retail and food service markets. Limited tickets remain to join the tour.
summary: About 40 Australian beef industry stakeholders will depart next August on a 12-day AWA study tour of Japan's Wagyu supply chain, with limited tickets remaining.

title: Hustler Launches Third-Gen Unrolla TX235 Bale Feeder
description_clean: Well known for its extensive range of feeding solutions, Hustler has introduced its third-generation Unrolla TX 235, two-bale, self-loading bale feeder. Design and engineered around getting feed to animals where they can eat it, and where wheels don’t run over it, the result is cleaner feed lines, resulting in less wastage and better utilisation of each bale. Said to offer the widest wheel track, that in turn helps lower the machine’s centre of gravity, offering improved stability, meaning TX 235 users can travel safely, even when delivering and feeding increasingly heavier bales in difficult
summary: Hustler has introduced its third-generation Unrolla TX 235 two-bale self-loading bale feeder, featuring a wide wheel track for improved stability and reduced feed wastage.

title: Fendt 900 Vario Marks 50,000 Units With Anniversary Edition
description_clean: Fendt's 900 Vario was unveiled at Agritechnica 1995 and has since gone on to become a popular choice for farmers and contractors worldwide. Having ticked over 50,000 examples over the last 30 years, a production run of just 300 limited edition series of tractors feature an engraved ‘50,000’ door sill, embroidered floor mats with an anniversary logo, super-comfort seats trimmed in titanium leather, and an anniversary badge on the chromed bonnet. The anniversary model reflects on some of the past models, with four colour options, including the trademark black of the 2005 model, alongside steel b
summary: Fendt is marking 50,000 units of its 900 Vario, launched in 1995, with a limited edition run of 300 anniversary tractors featuring special trim and badging.

title: New App StockScore Digitises Livestock Condition Scoring
description_clean: A new app designed in New Zealand aims to help sheep, beef and deer farmers capture, analyse and use body condition score data more effectively. StockScore, developed by Central Otago veterinarian, farmer and agri-entrepreneur Becks Smith, is designed specifically for use in the yards. Built for one-handed use and capable of working offline, the app allows farmers to record body condition scores as they work, removing the reliance on notebooks, memory or paper records that are often lost once the job is done. “Body condition scoring has been part of good livestock management for decades, but t
summary: StockScore, a New Zealand app developed by Central Otago vet and farmer Becks Smith, digitises livestock body condition scoring for sheep, beef and deer farmers with offline, one-handed use.

title: Andy Macfarlane Joins AgriZeroNZ Board
description_clean: Canterbury farmer Andy Macfarlane has been appointed to the board of AgriZeroNZ, the public-private partnership designed to accelerate the development and deployment of emissions reduction tools for New Zealand farmers. Rob Hewett, chair of AgriZeroNZ's board, says he welcomes Macfarlane, adding that he brings a combination of governance expertise, commercial acumen and hands-on farming experience. “Andy’s experience across a range of farming systems and value chains, combined with his commercial pragmatism and ability to connect market signals with on-farm decision-making will be of immense b
summary: Canterbury farmer Andy Macfarlane has been appointed to the AgriZeroNZ board, with chair Rob Hewett citing his governance expertise, commercial acumen and farming experience.

title: Bird Flu: Keep Distance, Wash Hands, Report It
description_clean: OPINION: It's a tough pill to swallow that bird flu (avian influenza) has finally arrived in New Zealand. This is a moment many of us have hoped would never come. We've watched the highly pathogenic H5 strain spread across Europe, Asia, the Americas and, more recently, Australia. With so many migratory birds travelling to and through New Zealand, experts have been warning since 2022 that it was only a matter of time before this strain reached our shores. On 15 July the virus was confirmed in a brown skua north of Wellington City, followed by a kāhu in Wairarapa. The good news is that, so far,
summary: An opinion piece responds to bird flu's arrival in New Zealand, confirmed in a brown skua near Wellington on 15 July and later a kāhu in Wairarapa, urging precautions such as keeping distance, washing hands and reporting cases.

title: Clostridial Disease in Lambs: NZ Vaccination Guide
description_clean: Although preventable, lamb loss from clostridial diseases during the first year of life on New Zealand hill country farms continues to be an issue. A field trial, where lambs were vaccinated at docking and weaning, demonstrated that vaccination was highly effective, lowering clostridial deaths by 23%. Zoetis veterinarian Dr Victoria Chapman unpacks the importance of robust animal health programmes, vaccination timing and the potential harmful consequences for stock when vaccination isn't given. What are the main clostridial diseases in New Zealand? The clostridial diseases that are most likely
summary: Zoetis vet Dr Victoria Chapman discusses clostridial diseases in New Zealand lambs, noting a field trial found vaccination at docking and weaning lowered clostridial deaths by 23%.

title: Apple sector veteran honoured for 'extraordinary contribution'
description_clean: For over 50 years Mel Ewers has been supporting the apple sector navigate through compliance and quality assurance issues. And Ewers’ contribution was recognised last night with the New Zealand Apples & Pears Incorporated (NZAPI) Outstanding Contribution Award at dinner in Queenstown. Around 260 people, who attended the function, heard that Ewers has quietly made an extraordinary contribution to the sector, supporting growers, packhouses, exporters, and industry organisations through her expertise in compliance, logistics, quality assurance, market access, training, and biosecurity. Throughout
summary: Mel Ewers, with over 50 years supporting the apple sector in compliance and quality assurance, received the NZAPI Outstanding Contribution Award at a Queenstown dinner attended by around 260 people.
